teachingPoint: T674 FramerTextElementFetcher fetches text component from Framer workspace via fabric abstraction, validates workspace ownership against tenantId. Framer stores text as base component + overrides; the fetcher retrieves both. This enables T675 to apply overrides correctly. Workspace isolation is enforced: tenantId must own workspace.
positiveExample: T674: @Inject(FRAMER_FABRIC) fabric; workspace = await fabric.getWorkspaceIfOwned(workspaceId, tenantId); component = await fabric.getTextComponent(componentId, workspace).
negativeExample: T674: Direct Framer SDK call without workspace ownership check. Any user can fetch any workspace's components.
discriminatingConstraint: T674 MUST validate workspace ownership against tenantId. MUST use fabric abstraction, not direct SDK.
keywords: FramerTextElementFetcher workspace ownership validation authorization fabric

teachingPoint: T675 TextVariantResolver resolves which variant of the base text component applies (e.g., 'h1', 'h2', 'body', 'caption'), then maps variant to canonical font size via FREEDOM config. Framer uses variant names; XIIGen uses canonical sizes (16px, 24px, etc.). The mapping is tenant-customizable via FREEDOM, not hardcoded.
positiveExample: T675: variant = component.variant ('h1'). fontSize = FREEDOM.variantToFontSize['h1'] ('32px'). Maps Framer variant → canonical size.
negativeExample: T675: Hardcodes 'h1' → 32px in code. Tenant cannot customize mapping.
discriminatingConstraint: Variant-to-font-size mappings MUST come from FREEDOM config. No hardcoded conditionals.
keywords: TextVariantResolver font size mapping variant configuration FREEDOM customizable

teachingPoint: T676 OverrideResolver applies component overrides (delta-style changes from base component) to the base component styles. Framer stores overrides as a partial object; the resolver merges them: base = {fontSize:16px, color:black}, override = {color:blue} → result = {fontSize:16px, color:blue}. This is a shallow merge; deep nesting checked by T677.
positiveExample: T676: base = component.baseStyle; overrides = component.overrides; merged = {...base, ...overrides}. Shallow merge, applied in order.
negativeExample: T676: Applies only override values, ignoring base. Result is incomplete.
discriminatingConstraint: Overrides MUST be merged with base styles. Shallow merge sufficient; deep nesting handled by StyleResolutionEngine.
keywords: OverrideResolver override merge base style delta shallow merge

teachingPoint: T677 StyleResolutionEngine resolves final computed style by combining base + overrides + variant properties. This is where all sources of style truth converge: variant fonts, base colors, override tweaks. The engine validates that all required properties (fontSize, color, lineHeight) are present; missing properties are filled from FREEDOM defaults.
positiveExample: T677: final = {...variantDefaults, ...baseStyle, ...overrides}; assert all required props present; fill missing from FREEDOM.defaults. Returns complete style record.
negativeExample: T677: Returns partially resolved style. Downstream must handle missing properties.
discriminatingConstraint: Final resolved style MUST contain all required properties (fontSize, color, lineHeight, etc.). Missing required property → error.
keywords: StyleResolutionEngine computed style final resolution required properties defaults

teachingPoint: T678 AnimationMetadataExcluder detects animation metadata in Framer components and excludes it from XIIGen output, recording the exclusion in xiigen-excluded-metadata audit index. Framer text components may have animations (fade-in, slide, scale); XIIGen's text adapter handles static text only. Animation metadata is recorded (not lost), but not exported.
positiveExample: T678: If component.animations exists, storeDocument('xiigen-excluded-metadata', {elementId, type:'animation', metadataSnapshot: component.animations}). Continue without animations.
negativeExample: T678: Exports animation metadata verbatim. Downstream text arbiters break on animation properties.
discriminatingConstraint: Animation metadata MUST be detected and excluded. Exclusion MUST be recorded in xiigen-excluded-metadata for visibility.
keywords: AnimationMetadataExcluder animation exclusion audit trail excluded-metadata

teachingPoint: T679 WorkspaceScopedAggregator combines all element data (variant, style, overrides, exclusions) with workspaceId context and enforces workspace isolation: all records must reference the same workspaceId. This prevents accidentally mixing components from different Framer workspaces, which would create invalid imports.
positiveExample: T679: Aggregates all elements with resolved styles. Before storing, asserts all records.workspaceId === batchWorkspaceId. Frame isolation validated.
negativeExample: T679: Stores components without workspace validation. Later, query mixes workspaces; downstream breaks.
discriminatingConstraint: All components in a batch MUST reference the same workspaceId. Workspace isolation validated before storage.
keywords: WorkspaceScopedAggregator workspace isolation consistency validation batching
